rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Use only identifiers and types present in the analyzed AADL model; avoid introducing new package-qualified names.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structural integrity by aligning AGREE contracts with component type and implementation scopes.
Ensure all AGREE expressions reference only visible AADL ports, data types, and properties from the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports in valid scope; implementation bindings use subcomponent ports.
anti_patterns: Do not place AGREE contracts in invalid AADL scopes such as outside component types or implementations.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and structure in component type vs implementation annexes
AADL property associations and timing constraints
Defensive rules for type mismatches and invented constraints
